mechanism_narrative: A sustained federal funds rate below 0.25 percent suppresses the discount rate on future corporate cash flows and stimulates risk appetite, generating positive S&P 500 forward returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are not positive in more than 55 percent of observations when FEDFUNDS is below 0.25 percent.

mechanism_narrative: A month-over-month decline in the unemployment rate of at least 0.2 percentage points signals accelerating labor market absorption, lifting corporate revenue expectations and driving positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: S&P 500 posts negative 20-day returns in over 50 percent of instances following a 0.2 percentage point monthly decline in UNRATE.

mechanism_narrative: Joint positive monthly changes in retail sales and industrial production indicate broad demand-supply alignment that raises earnings expectations and produces positive S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are not positive in at least 60 percent of months when both RSAFS and INDPRO post monthly increases.

mechanism_narrative: Consumer price inflation accelerating above 2 percent while the federal funds rate remains below 0.5 percent signals stagflation risk that compresses real earnings and triggers negative S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
regime_hint: zero lower bound with inflation acceleration
failure_condition: S&P 500 20-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 45 percent of triggered instances.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month change in industrial production signals expanding capacity utilization and operating leverage gains, producing positive S&P 500 returns over the next 5 trading days.
failure_condition: 5-day forward returns are not positive in more than 55 percent of weeks when INDPRO posts a monthly increase.

mechanism_narrative: Housing starts exceeding 1.1 million units signals durable demand strength that spills over into construction and consumer spending, lifting S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in more than 40 percent of triggered quarters when HOUST exceeds 1100.

mechanism_narrative: Positive month-over-month change in nonfarm payroll employment reflects rising aggregate income and consumption demand, generating positive S&P 500 returns over the next 20 trading days.
failure_condition: 20-day forward returns are not positive in more than 55 percent of months when PAYEMS increases.

mechanism_narrative: Contraction in real GDP coincident with a rising unemployment rate triggers a recession pricing channel that drives negative S&P 500 returns over the next 60 trading days.
candidate_trigger: GDPC1 quarter-over-quarter growth negative and UNRATE increasing month-over-month
failure_condition: 60-day forward returns are negative in fewer than 50 percent of triggered quarters when GDPC1 declines and UNRATE rises.
